description: Phase 0 connect-via-tool tests. Each test asks the agent to connect to the Dataverse org using ONE of the four tool surfaces (PAC CLI, Python SDK, Dataverse MCP, raw Web API) and read 1 record from the `systemuser` table. systemuser is always populated (the calling SPN itself), so reads can never fail due to seed data being absent. Runs in BOTH sim and live: sim grades the plugin's documented pattern for that tool; live grades a real round-trip against a test org. Soft P1 (did the work happen?), rich P2/P3 (did it happen well?).

tests.prompt: Read `./SETUP.md` first if it exists. Use ONLY the PAC CLI to verify connectivity to the Dataverse org. Run a `pac org` read-only command (such as `pac org who`) and report: (1) the connected organization URL, (2) the connected user/SPN id. The `eval` profile is already created and selected. Do not call the Python SDK, raw Web API, or MCP tools.
tests.expected_response: Agent runs `pac org who` (or equivalent), parses the output, reports OrgUrl + Connected user. No SDK/MCP/Web-API.
tests.description: Connect via PAC CLI — verify org connection + read identity
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent invokes a `pac org` read-only command (e.g. `pac org who`) and reports the connected organization URL and user/SPN id successfully.
PRIORITY_1: Agent uses PAC CLI for this command-style operation (not SDK, not Web API, not MCP).
PRIORITY_1: Agent's executable code is shell/PAC commands — Python wrappers around `pac` are fine, but inline Python that calls into SDK instead = FAIL.
PRIORITY_2: Agent does NOT call `pac auth create` (env is pre-authed; recreating the profile is an anti-pattern).
PRIORITY_2: Agent runs ≤3 commands to complete the task (no exploratory probing of `pac help`, `pac auth list`, etc. when the goal is clear).
PRIORITY_2: Agent demonstrates understanding of when PAC is the right tool — references admin/env operations or that PAC is the documented choice for org-level reads.
PRIORITY_3: Agent parses structured fields from `pac org who` output (OrgUrl, Connected as) rather than dumping raw text.
PRIORITY_3: Agent acknowledges the `eval` profile is pre-selected (e.g. checks `pac auth list` once at most, or skips entirely).

tests.prompt: Read `./SETUP.md` and `./connect.py` first if they exist. Use ONLY the Python SDK (`DataverseClient`) to read up to 3 records from the `systemuser` table and print each record's `fullname`. The systemuser table is always populated (the calling SPN itself is a systemuser), so you should always see at least one row. Use the documented auth pattern: `from auth import get_credential, load_env`. The pre-provisioned `scripts/auth.py` returns a working credential. Do not call PAC CLI, raw Web API, or MCP tools.
tests.expected_response: Agent imports `from auth import get_credential, load_env`, constructs `DataverseClient`, calls `client.records.get('systemuser', select=['fullname'])`, iterates the first page, and prints fullname values.
tests.description: Connect via Python SDK — DataverseClient + auth.py + records.get
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent uses the official Python SDK (`DataverseClient.records.get` or equivalent). Hand-rolled urllib/requests for a simple read = FAIL.
PRIORITY_1: Agent's executable code is Python (no JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js or PowerShell).
PRIORITY_1: CONTAINS: systemuser
PRIORITY_1: CONTAINS: DataverseClient

PRIORITY_2: Agent uses the documented auth pattern (`get_credential()` from `scripts/auth.py`) rather than re-implementing CertificateCredential, DefaultAzureCredential, or manual `pkcs12.load_key_and_certificates` chains.
PRIORITY_2: Agent uses `client.records.get(...)` (page-iterator, auto-paginated) — NOT a manually constructed OData $top/$skip URL.
PRIORITY_2: Agent's script is concise (≤30 lines for the connect+read; no excessive logging or defensive try/except around every step).
PRIORITY_3: Agent uses `select=['fullname']` (or another column projection) rather than fetching all columns.
PRIORITY_3: SKILL_LOADED: dv-connect

tests.prompt: Read `./SETUP.md` first if it exists. The Dataverse MCP server is already loaded into Copilot CLI (you should see tools namespaced as `dataverse-*` in your tool list — e.g. `dataverse-describe_table`, `dataverse-list_tables`, `dataverse-read_query`). Use ONLY the MCP tool surface to read 1 record from the `systemuser` table — pick whichever MCP tool fits best (read_query, list_records, etc.) and report the fullname of the first record. Do not call the Python SDK, PAC CLI, or raw Web API; do NOT call the `/api/mcp` HTTP endpoint manually.
tests.expected_response: Agent invokes a `dataverse-*` MCP tool to read systemuser, parses the response, reports the first fullname. No SDK/PAC/Web-API.
tests.description: Connect via Dataverse MCP — invoke a dataverse-* tool to read 1 systemuser
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent invokes a `dataverse-*` MCP tool (such as `dataverse-read_query`, `dataverse-list_records`, etc.) and successfully reports the fullname of at least one systemuser record.
PRIORITY_1: Agent uses the MCP tool surface — does NOT manually call `/api/mcp` via urllib/requests, does NOT use SDK/PAC for this read.
PRIORITY_2: Agent does NOT spend time inspecting `mcp-config.json` or trying to authenticate to MCP manually — the server is already loaded by Copilot CLI.
PRIORITY_2: Agent picks the most appropriate MCP tool for the task (read_query/list_records for a row read, NOT describe_table or list_tables which return metadata only).
PRIORITY_2: Agent's reasoning explicitly notes that MCP is the right tool for small bounded reads (or doesn't reason about tool choice at all — both are fine; explicit wrong reasoning = FAIL).
PRIORITY_3: SKILL_LOADED: dv-connect

tests.prompt: Read `./SETUP.md` and `./connect.py` first if they exist. Use ONLY the raw Web API (via `urllib.request`) to call `/api/data/v9.2/systemusers?$top=1&$select=fullname` and print the first record's fullname. Use `auth.get_token()` from the pre-provisioned `scripts/auth.py` to obtain the bearer token. Do not call the Python SDK, PAC CLI, or MCP tools (this is a deliberate Web-API-only test of raw HTTP plumbing, not a routing test).
tests.expected_response: Agent imports `from auth import get_token, load_env`, builds the systemusers OData URL with $top=1&$select=fullname, sets Authorization: Bearer header, parses JSON response, prints fullname.
tests.description: Connect via raw Web API — urllib + Bearer token + OData query
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent uses `urllib.request` with a Bearer token from `auth.get_token()` to call `/api/data/v9.2/systemusers` and prints at least one fullname successfully.
PRIORITY_1: CONTAINS: urllib
PRIORITY_1: CONTAINS: systemusers
PRIORITY_1: CONTAINS_ANY: get_token, Authorization
PRIORITY_2: Agent uses the pre-provisioned `scripts/auth.py` (does not re-implement OAuth, MSAL, or manual token endpoint POSTs).
PRIORITY_2: Agent uses OData query parameters (`$top=1`, `$select=fullname`) to limit the response — does NOT fetch all rows or all columns.

PRIORITY_2: Agent acknowledges that raw Web API is a tier-2 fallback (acceptable here because the prompt forces it; the agent should not advocate Web API as a general approach for SDK-supported reads).
PRIORITY_3: Agent handles HTTP errors with at least one try/except around the `urlopen` call.
PRIORITY_3: SKILL_LOADED: dv-connect
